GroupResponseGroupData.description: Information about objects matching the given key.

aggregation_count.description:
Number of objects in group.
This field is always set.

aggregation_uuids.description:
List of UUIDs of the objects in the group.
Only populated if show_aggregation_uuids is set.

aggregation_value.description: Value of the aggregation operation as calculated by the query.

unique_counts.description:
Map of counts for the given unique_count_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_count_paths is set.

unique_values.description:
Map of values for the given unique_value_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_value_paths is set.

PackageManagerAWSAuthProvider.description:
AWSAuthProvider represents a configuration for the authentication with the
AWS Code Artifact repository.

allowed_audience.description: allowed_audience is the allowed audience value to retrieve the role.

domain.description: domain name of the AWS Code Artifact repository.

domain_owner.description: domain_owner of the AWS Code Artifact repository.

region.description: region is the region of the AWS Code Artifact repository.

repository.description: repositiry of the AWS Code Artifact repository.

target_role_arn.description: target_role_arn is the role that we need to assume in AWS.

PackageManagerGARAuthProvider.description:
GARAuthProvider represents a configuration for authentication with
Google Artifact Registry.
Supported package manager types: NPM, MVN, GRADLE, PYPI.
Unsupported types (CARGO, GEM, NUGET, PACKAGIST, COCOAPOD, SWIFT)
are rejected at runtime.

location.description:
location is the GCP region or multi-region where the repository is hosted
(e.g., "us-central1", "europe-west1", "us").

project_id.description: project_id is the GCP project ID that hosts the Artifact Registry repository.

repository.description: repository is the name of the Artifact Registry repository.

service_account_key.description:
service_account_key is the JSON key for a GCP service account with
artifactregistry.reader permissions. Encrypted at rest.

PackageManagerServiceCreatePackageManage.description:
PackageManager holds the data for the various package
managers of the enterprise. Mainly locations and
access credentials when needed to resolve/scan packages
from private package managers.
An enterprise can create one or more package managers
in a namespace. All the package managers of the given
namespace will be taken into account when resolving.

meta.description: Metadata is generic for any object.

propagate.description: Propagate indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

uuid.description: The UUID of the package manager.

PackageManagerServiceUpdatePackageManage.description: Request to update the package manager.

object.description:
PackageManager holds the data for the various package
managers of the enterprise. Mainly locations and
access credentials when needed to resolve/scan packages
from private package managers.
An enterprise can create one or more package managers
in a namespace. All the package managers of the given
namespace will be taken into account when resolving.

meta.description: Metadata is generic for any object.

propagate.description: Propagate indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

uuid.description: The UUID of the package manager.

last_tested_at.description: last_tested_at is the time when the package manager was last tested.

state.description: The state of the package manager.

SpecPackageManagerStatusState.description: The status of the package manager.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

v1CargoSpec.description: CargoSpec represents a configuration for Cargo.

priority.description:
The priority of Cargo.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

token.description: A token that must be provided by the repository owner.

url.description: The url of the crates registry that Cargo must use.

v1CocoapodSpec.description:
Represents a configuration for the Cocoapod package manager.
Cocoapods does not support any registry authentication.

priority.description:
The priority of the Cocoapod package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the Cocoapods package manager must use.

v1CountResponse.description: Response to a list count request.

count.description: Number of objects matching the given list parameters.

v1GemSpec.description: GemSpec represents a configuration for the Ruby gems package manager.

password.description: An encrypted password that must be provided by the repository owner.

priority.description:
The priority of the Ruby gems package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the Ruby gems package manager must use.

user.description: The user name to use to access this repository.

v1GradleSpec.description: GradleSpec represents properties that need to be set for Gradle projects.

mtls.description: MTLS authentication for the Gradle package manager.

property_key_name.description: The key name for Gradle properties.

property_key_value.description: The key value for Gradle properties.

v1GroupAggregationValueResponse.description:
GroupAggregationValueResponse returns the value of the aggregation if
requested.

v1GroupResponse.description: Response to a list group request.

v1IndexData.description:
IndexData is used to index the resource for search. It's an internal
object.

search_score.description: search_score is the score of the resource for search. Internal use only.

will_be_deleted_at.description: Time that the resource will be deleted.

v1ListPackageManagersResponse.description: Response to the request to list package managers.

next_page_id.description: The UUID of last message retrieved.

next_page_token.description: Pagination token that can be used to retrieve the next page of results.

ca_cert.description: The CA certificate in PEM format.

client_cert.description: The client certificate in PEM format.

client_key.description: The client key in PEM format.

server_cert.description: The server certificate in PEM format.

v1MavenSpec.description: MavenSpec represents a configuration for the Maven package manager.

id.description:
A string that uniquely identifies this proxy/server. This is the
UUID of the object if not provided.

is_plugin_repository.description: Indicates whether the repository is a plugin or binary repository.

mtls.description: MTLS authentication for the Maven package manager.

name.description:
User identifiable name for the repository. This is the name
of the package manager if not provided.

password.title:
An encrypted password that must be provided by the repository owner.
https://maven.apache.org/guides/mini/guide-encryption.html

priority.description:
The priority of the Maven package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

public_repository.description: Indicates if cloud storage is accessible publicly.

region.title: Region to be used for cloud storage integration

snapshots.description: Indicates whether the repository can contain snapshot versions.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the Maven package manager must use.

user.description: The user name to use to access this repo.

v1Meta.description: Common fields for all Endor Labs resources.

annotations.description:
Annotations can be used to attach metadata to a resource message.
Annotation values can be small or large, structured or unstructured,
and may include characters not permitted by labels.
The keys may contain alphanumerics, underscores (_), dots (.) and dashes
(-). The values of an annotation must be 16384 bytes or smaller.

create_time.description:
Time the resource was created.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

created_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the user who created the object,
for example, ewok@endor.ai@google@api-key.

description.description: Resource description. Must be less than 1024 bytes.

kind.description:
Resource kind, for example, HelloResponse.
Auto-generated using the protobuf message proto.MessageName().Name().

name.description: Resource name. Must be 63 characters or less.

parent_kind.description: Parent object resource kind, for example, Project.

references.description: Map of objects referenced in a query API.

tags.description:
List of tags attached to the resource.
Tags can be used to select objects and to find collections of objects that
satisfy certain conditions. A tag must be 255 characters or less.

update_time.description:
Time the resource was last updated.
Note: Updated on all create/patch/delete operations.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

updated_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the last user who updated the object,
for example, vulnerabilityingestor@endor.ai@x509.

upsert_time.description:
Time the resource was last upserted.

Note:
create_time is only set the first time the resource is created.
upsert_time is set every time the resource is upseted.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

v1NPMSpec.description: NPMSpec represents a configuration for the Node package manager.

auth_token.description:
An auth token that cloud providers typically provide to connect to the
registry.

mtls.description: MTLS authentication for the NPM package manager.

priority.description:
The priority of the Node package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

scope.title:
A private registry scope.
Ex: @myorg/pkg
myorg is the scope

token.description: A base64 encoded user name and password as token.

url.description: The URL of the registry that NPM must use.

v1NugetSpec.description: NugetSpec represents a configuration for the NuGet package manager.

password.description: An encrypted password that must be provided by the repository owner.

priority.description:
The priority of the NuGet package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the NuGet package manager must use.

user.description: The username to use to access this repository.

v1PackageManager.description:
PackageManager holds the data for the various package
managers of the enterprise. Mainly locations and
access credentials when needed to resolve/scan packages
from private package managers.
An enterprise can create one or more package managers
in a namespace. All the package managers of the given
namespace will be taken into account when resolving.

meta.description: Metadata is generic for any object.

propagate.description: Propagate indicates that the object should be visible in child namespaces.

uuid.description: The UUID of the package manager.

auth_provider.description:
AuthProvider is the authentication provider for the package manager
that must be used.

v1PackagistSpec.description: Represents a configuration for the Packagist(PHP) package manager.

host.description:
The host name of the registry or repository where the PHP packages are
hosted.

password.description: An encrypted password that must be provided by the repository owner.

user.description: The user name to use to access the given host.

v1PypiSpec.description: PypiSpec represents a configuration for the Python package manager.

mtls.description: MTLS authentication for the Python package manager.

password.description: An encrypted password that must be provided by the repository owner.

priority.description:
The priority of the Python package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the Python package manager must use.

user.description: The user name to use to access this repository.

v1SwiftSpec.description: Represents a configuration for the Swift package manager.

basic_auth.description: Basic auth credentials to authenticate to the registry.

priority.description:
The priority of the Swift package manager.
Priorities are relative. If multiple package managers
have the same priority, the order between them can be random.
Lower number means higher priority.

token.description: Access token to authenticate to the registry.

url.description: The URL of the registry that the Swift package manager must use.

v1TenantMeta.description: Tenant related data for the tenant containing the resource.

namespace.description:
Namespaces are a way to organize organizational units into virtual
groupings of resources. Namespaces must be a fully qualified name,
for example, the child namespace of namespace "endor.prod" called "app"
is called "endor.prod.app".

v1UpdateRequest.description: Message used for all update requests.

force.description:
Force will force the update of the resource if any
checks fail.

update_mask.description: Fields to update. Defaults to all fields.
